title: myosotis scorpioides subsp. radicans
intro: Myosotis scorpioides subsp. radicans, also known as creeping forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the reduction of swelling. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation due to its sedative properties, and it has been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Myosotis scorpioides subsp. radicans can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
